purpose: Derive a reviewable current queue view from the immutable operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json snapshot and additive owner-scoped operations/queue-updates/*.json records without deleting, overwriting, or silently resolving contradictory history.

repository_history.authority: temporal ordering only; never causal proof

normalization.identity_key: item_id, falling back to id only for baseline items
normalization.tie_breaker: git commit ancestry, then lexical path; ties remain explicit conflicts

validation_rules.rule: An owner-scoped update may change only an item whose owner matches the update owner.
Every derived field must retain source path, blob SHA when available, commit SHA when available, and source timestamp.
A later record supersedes a prior current-state value but never removes the prior record from history.
Two incomparable records that assign different normalized statuses to the same item produce a conflict, not an arbitrary winner.
A completed item may have a handoff proposing a new item; the handoff does not reopen the completed item unless an explicit later update says so.
Commit order establishes before/after only and must not be represented as proof that one artifact caused another.

derivation_algorithm: Parse the baseline snapshot and emit one observed historical record per item.
Parse every owner-scoped update; reject malformed or owner-mismatched records into an error stream without deleting them.
Group valid records by item identity.
Order records using explicit record timestamps, then commit ancestry, then lexical path.
Normalize status aliases while retaining the original status string.
Mark each earlier comparable state as superseded by the later state; retain both records.
When ordering is incomplete or states disagree across incomparable records, emit an unresolved conflict and do not choose a current status.
Emit a derived current view, complete history, provenance graph, validation errors, and unresolved conflicts as separate collections.

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is older than multiple owner-scoped queue-update artifacts.
Completed continuity records M-001 through M-004 preserve handoffs for subsequent work without reopening the completed records.
The repository already follows an append-only evidence and queue-update pattern.
inferred: The aggregate queue functions as a baseline snapshot rather than a continuously authoritative current-state document.
Current state should be computed, not manually overwritten, to preserve cross-team provenance.
proposed: Implement an executable reconciler and deterministic fixtures from this contract.
Publish derived queue views as generated artifacts, never as replacements for source history.
superseded: Treating operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone as the complete live queue is superseded by the later additive update model.
unresolved: No executable reconciler currently proves behavior against malformed timestamps, owner mismatches, or incomparable commit histories.
No canonical generated-current-view path has been selected.

acceptance_tests_for_implementation: A later completed update supersedes an active baseline state while both remain in history.
An owner mismatch is rejected and surfaced without mutating the derived item.
Two incomparable conflicting updates produce an unresolved conflict.
A handoff on a completed item does not reopen that item.
Every derived current field has source and commit provenance.
Running the reconciler twice over identical inputs produces byte-identical canonical JSON.
